title: ColdRelay vs Microsoft 365 — Cold Email Infrastructure Comparison
description: Comparing ColdRelay's dedicated cold email infrastructure with Microsoft 365 for outbound campaigns. See pricing, deliverability, setup complexity, and risk side-by-side.

overview.coldrelay: ColdRelay provisions Microsoft 365 mailboxes on dedicated, isolated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs — purpose-built for cold email outbound. Pricing starts at $1/mailbox/month with volume discounts down to $0.55 at scale. Each deployment includes auto-configured DNS (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), 100-150 mailboxes per domain, no warmup requirement, and a 99% inbox placement guarantee with a money-back promise. Setup completes in 2-4 hours.
overview.competitor: Microsoft 365 is Microsoft's cloud productivity suite, with Exchange Online providing business email. Plans start at $6/user/month (Business Basic) and go up to $22/user/month (Business Premium). Microsoft 365 offers Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and the full Office suite. While technically capable of sending cold email, Microsoft monitors for bulk outreach and may throttle or suspend accounts that exhibit cold email patterns.

featureComparison.coldrelay: Dedicated cold email infrastructure — optimized for outbound deliverability
featureComparison.competitor: Business productivity and communication suite — cold email is a grey area at best

featureComparison.coldrelay: Fully isolated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs — your reputation is completely separate
featureComparison.competitor: Shared tenant infrastructure unless you set up your own Azure tenant manually

featureComparison.coldrelay: Fully automated SPF, DKIM, DMARC — configured during setup
featureComparison.competitor: Manual configuration required through Microsoft 365 admin center and DNS provider

featureComparison.coldrelay: 2-4 hours for complete infrastructure including DNS propagation
featureComparison.competitor: Hours to days for manual tenant creation, domain verification, DNS configuration, and mailbox provisioning

featureComparison.coldrelay: No warmup needed — infrastructure is pre-optimized for day-1 sending
featureComparison.competitor: New accounts need gradual warmup to build sender reputation (2-4 weeks typical)

featureComparison.coldrelay: 100-150 mailboxes per domain — dense utilization for cost efficiency
featureComparison.competitor: No hard limit, but sending patterns from many mailboxes on one domain trigger Microsoft's abuse detection

featureComparison.coldrelay: Zero — cold email is the intended use case
featureComparison.competitor: Moderate — Microsoft can throttle sending or disable accounts that trigger bulk email heuristics

featureComparison.coldrelay: Order 50 to 5,000+ mailboxes; volume pricing gets cheaper as you grow
featureComparison.competitor: Each user needs individual licensing; no volume discount for cold email use cases
Full Office suite: Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive

featureComparison.coldrelay: Fully managed — ColdRelay handles tenant, domain, and mailbox administration
featureComparison.competitor: Self-managed — you handle tenant admin, licensing, DNS, security policies, and compliance

sections.coldrelayWins: ColdRelay essentially packages Microsoft 365 infrastructure in a way that's optimized, isolated, and priced for cold email. You get the same underlying Exchange/Outlook technology, but with dedicated Azure tenants, dedicated IPs, and auto-configured DNS — all at $1/mailbox instead of $6-$22/user. The fully managed experience means no manual tenant administration, no DNS troubleshooting, and no warmup period. At 500 mailboxes, ColdRelay costs $425/month vs. $3,000+ on Microsoft 365 direct — a 7x savings minimum. The isolation is the real differentiator: shared Microsoft 365 tenants mean your sending reputation is affected by other users. ColdRelay's isolated tenants keep your reputation entirely in your control.
sections.competitorWins: Microsoft 365 is the industry standard for business productivity. If your team needs Outlook desktop apps, Teams for video conferencing, SharePoint for document management, or the full Office suite, Microsoft 365 delivers unmatched value. For your team's primary business email — client communication, internal collaboration, and everyday work — Microsoft 365 is reliable, secure, and deeply integrated into enterprise workflows. The admin center gives you granular control over security policies, compliance, and data governance.
sections.whoShouldChooseColdRelay: Choose ColdRelay if you need email infrastructure specifically for cold outreach. If you're managing outbound campaigns with dozens to thousands of sending accounts, ColdRelay gives you the same Microsoft 365 email technology at a fraction of the cost, with dedicated infrastructure and zero suspension risk. It's the right tool for agencies, sales teams, and lead gen operations that need reliable, affordable sending infrastructure.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Microsoft 365 for your team's primary business accounts — the ones used for client meetings, document collaboration, and day-to-day communication. Microsoft 365 is a productivity platform, not a cold email tool. If you need the Office suite, Teams, or enterprise compliance features, Microsoft 365 is the obvious choice for those needs.
sections.verdict: ColdRelay and Microsoft 365 serve different purposes even though they share the same underlying Exchange technology. Microsoft 365 is the best business productivity suite available, but it's expensive and risky for cold email. ColdRelay takes that same technology and packages it at $1/mailbox with dedicated infrastructure — purpose-built for outbound. Use Microsoft 365 for productivity, ColdRelay for sending.

faqs.question: Does ColdRelay use Microsoft 365 infrastructure?
faqs.answer: Yes. ColdRelay provisions mailboxes on Microsoft's Azure/Exchange infrastructure, but on dedicated, isolated tenants with dedicated IPs. You get the same email technology with better isolation and pricing for cold email use cases.

faqs.question: Can Microsoft 365 suspend my account for cold email?
faqs.answer: Yes. Microsoft monitors sending patterns and can throttle or disable accounts that exhibit bulk outreach behavior. While enforcement is less aggressive than Google, the risk is real — especially at scale.

faqs.question: Why not just set up my own Azure tenant for cold email?
faqs.answer: You can, but you'd need to handle tenant creation, domain configuration, DNS setup, IP reputation management, and ongoing maintenance yourself. ColdRelay automates all of this and does it at a lower per-mailbox cost due to volume arrangements.

faqs.question: Is ColdRelay cheaper than buying Microsoft 365 licenses directly?
faqs.answer: Significantly. ColdRelay starts at $1/mailbox vs. Microsoft 365's minimum of $6/user. At 1,000 mailboxes, you'd pay $700/month on ColdRelay vs. $6,000+/month on Microsoft 365 direct.

faqs.question: Can I use both ColdRelay and Microsoft 365?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — and that's the recommended approach. Keep Microsoft 365 for your team's primary email and productivity needs. Use ColdRelay for your dedicated cold email sending infrastructure. This separation protects your primary domain's reputation.
